specification.topic-statement: The fork event persistence system builds and inserts database records for both sides of a fork operation, capturing a snapshot of the child agent's pinned documents and toolset configuration.

scope.in-scope: Mapping of thinking level enum values to their canonical string representations
Construction of a human-readable fork feedback message
Insertion of paired parent and child fork event records into the database
Snapshot of pinned document paths and toolset filter at the moment of forking
scope.boundary: Spawning the child agent process or initializing its state is out of scope.
Parsing the user's fork command arguments is covered separately.
Managing pinned documents beyond snapshotting them at fork time is out of scope.

field.name: child_uuid
field.notes: UUID of the newly created child agent

field.name: fork_message_id
field.notes: ID of the message that triggered the fork

field.name: fork_message_id
field.notes: ID of the message that triggered the fork

field.name: pinned_paths
field.notes: paths of all currently pinned documents on the child agent at fork time

field.name: toolset_filter
field.notes: names of all tools in the child agent's toolset filter; empty array when no filter is set

behavior.thinking-level-mapping: Four thinking level values are mapped to strings for use in human-readable output and database records: the minimum level maps to "min", low maps to "low", medium maps to "medium", and high maps to "high".
behavior.fork-feedback-message: After a fork completes, a human-readable feedback string is produced describing the newly created child. The string takes the form: "Forked child {uuid} ({provider}/{model}/{thinking_level_string})" where the thinking level is rendered using the four-value mapping above.
behavior.parent-event-insertion: A fork event record is inserted into the database on behalf of the parent agent. The record's kind is "fork" and its agent field identifies the parent. The auxiliary JSON data encodes the child's UUID, the ID of the message that triggered the fork, and the role string "parent".
behavior.child-event-insertion: A second fork event record is inserted into the database on behalf of the child agent. The record's kind is "fork" and its agent field identifies the child. The auxiliary JSON data encodes the parent's UUID, the same triggering message ID, and the role string "child". It also includes two snapshot arrays captured from the child agent's state at the moment of forking.
behavior.pinned-paths-snapshot: The child event record includes a JSON array named "pinned_paths" containing one string entry per currently pinned document on the child agent. Each entry is the path of that pinned document. The array preserves all pinned paths as they exist at fork time and serves as a durable record of the child's initial document context.
behavior.toolset-filter-snapshot: The child event record includes a JSON array named "toolset_filter" containing one string entry per tool name present in the child agent's toolset filter. When no toolset filter has been configured, the array is empty. This snapshot captures the tool restrictions active on the child at the moment of forking.
behavior.insertion-result: The operation returns success if both event records are inserted without error, or an error if either insertion fails. No partial-success state is defined; a failure on either record propagates as the return value.
